title: Code Maintenance Domain Entry
use_for: finding root causes in unfamiliar or changing code
removing residual code after feature deletion
auditing hidden stale dependencies and coupling
do_not_use_for: new feature design without maintenance or correctness pressure
performance optimization as the primary goal
UI architecture as the primary owner
mod loader integration as the primary owner
assembly reverse engineering as the primary owner

primary_when: request is primarily about finding, explaining, or fixing broken behavior, regressions, failing tests, runtime errors, or incorrect state
request is primarily about refactoring existing code, reducing overengineering, removing stale code, or cleaning hidden coupling
must_not_own_when: request is primarily about a specialized domain and maintenance is only an implementation detail
request is about adding a new capability with no bug, regression, cleanup, or refactor concern

references.title: Bug Investigation
references.interpretation: Use for root-cause investigation, reproduction, narrowing, and validation.

references.title: Refactoring
references.interpretation: Use for scoped refactors, simplification, stale-code removal, and hidden-coupling audits.
